title: About
text:
I am a Master’s student in Business Analytics at Dublin City University. My interest in business analytics stems from a fascination with using data to support strategic decision-making and generate clear, valuable business insights.
During this course, I have focused on areas such as predictive modelling, data analysis, and business performance evaluation. Alongside this, my reflective and written work has deepened my interest in how people think, make decisions, respond to environments, and engage with organisations, products, and wider social issues.


I previously completed a BA in Business Studies International at Dublin City University, where I developed a strong foundation in finance, economics, operations, and strategy. During this time, I also studied abroad at the Autonomous University of Barcelona, which gave me valuable international experience and broadened my cultural awareness.
My background across business, analytics, and reflection allows me to approach problems from both a technical and strategic perspective.




Programming & Tools: Python, SQL, Excel, Power BI

Analytics: Data analysis, statistical modelling, data visualisation

Business Skills: Problem-solving, communication, teamwork, strategic thinking




I am particularly interested in: - business analytics and consulting
- data-driven strategy
- consumer behaviour and decision-making
- machine learning applications
- solving complex business challenges



Alongside my studies, I have worked in a fast-paced operational role at Gate Group Dublin, where I developed strong teamwork, attention to detail, and time management skills.
My academic and reflective work has also helped me think more deeply about communication, judgement, collaboration, and how analytical thinking can be applied in real-world contexts.



You can view my projects, reflections, and experience here:
View Projects View Reflections View CV

title: Hi, I’m Olive Edeko
text:
I am a Master’s student in Business Analytics at Dublin City University, with a strong interest in data-driven decision-making, predictive modelling, and strategic analysis.
My work focuses on using data to uncover insights, support business decisions, and solve real-world problems.

title: Strategic Foresight and Forecasting
text: This report explores the long-term future of Gort, County Galway, through the year 2060. Using a combination of foresight (scenario exploration) and forecasting (trend analysis), the project examines Gort’s potential evolution in terms of population, economy, and digitalisation to support resilient strategic decision-making for policymakers.

title: Fraud Pattern Recognition and Mitigation Analysis
text:
Tools Used
Data Processing: Excel was used for data cleaning, including rounding age variables and standardizing numerical features like credit scores and income for analysis. Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA): Python was employed to generate visualizations such as histograms and correlation matrices to identify trends. Statistical Techniques: Applied descriptive statistics and outlier flagging to isolate high-risk “extreme” transactions.

title: Predicting Business Star Ratings Using the Official Yelp Dataset
text:
Download Report


This project examines the Official Yelp Dataset to determine how business-level data, such as categories and specific attributes, can predict a business’s star rating.
Applying a Challenge Based Learning framework, the project proposes a niche analytics service to help businesses improve their reputation and visibility. It also explores how these insights can support Yelp’s internal operational decisions, such as identifying “Best of Yelp” candidates.



The chart below recreates one of the key insights from my analysis of the Yelp dataset.



The chart below recreates one of the key insights from my analysis of the Yelp dataset, showing how average ratings differ across business categories.









This visualisation is a simplified recreation of the analysis presented in the full report.
The results suggest that certain categories, such as cafes and hotels, tend to achieve higher average ratings, while others such as bars show slightly lower ratings. This highlights how customer expectations, service type, and experience consistency can vary across business categories and influence overall performance on platforms like Yelp.




Data Engineering: Processed raw data into 12 key categories and 33 primary attributes.
Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA): Used Python to visualise market saturation and identify patterns in star distribution.
Predictive Modelling: Compared three regression techniques:

Linear Regression: Established a baseline for variable relationships.
Random Forest Regressor: Selected for its ability to handle non-linear data and noise.
Gradient Boosting Regressor: Used to maximise accuracy through iterative error correction.





This project demonstrated how structured business data can be used to predict customer ratings and generate practical recommendations. It highlights the potential for analytics to improve business visibility, customer perception, and platform-level decision-making.
← Back to Projects

title: Gort 2060: Strategic Foresight and Forecasting
text:
Download Report


This report explores the long-term future of Gort, County Galway, through the year 2060.
Using a combination of foresight and forecasting, the project examines Gort’s potential evolution in terms of population, economy, and digitalisation to support resilient strategic decision-making for policymakers.



The chart below recreates the scenario-based structure used in the Gort 2060 project by comparing the three future pathways developed in the report.









This visualisation is a simplified recreation of the scenario-based analysis presented in the full report.
The Gort 2060 project used forecasting and foresight methods to explore how the town might develop under different future conditions. The best-case scenario reflects stronger digital integration, stable investment, and improved resilience, while the worst-case scenario reflects weaker coordination, lower investment, and greater long-term challenges. Presenting the scenarios visually helps show how strategic choices made today can shape very different outcomes for the future of Gort.




Horizon Scanning and Driver Mapping: Identified signals of change and mapped the primary drivers influencing the town’s development.
Axes of Uncertainty: Used a framework to define the most critical and unpredictable factors facing the region.
Scenario Development: Created three distinct pathways—Best-Case, Worst-Case, and Business-as-Usual—to visualise Gort’s future state.




The project identified key growth assets, including Gort’s digital hub and cultural diversity, while also highlighting major challenges such as commercial vacancy and transport constraints. The final scenarios provided a roadmap for improving Gort’s competitiveness and showed that early, coordinated action and investment in youth opportunities are essential for long-term regional resilience.
← Back to Projects

title: HealthPlus International: Global Sales & Profitability Audit
text:
Download Report


This project provides a comprehensive performance audit for HealthPlus International, a global entity operating across five regions.
The analysis evaluates revenue, profit margins, and sales channel efficiency across ten product categories to identify high-growth opportunities and address operational inefficiencies caused by the COVID-19 pandemic.



The chart below recreates one of the main regional insights from the HealthPlus International audit by comparing profit margin performance across key regions.









This visualisation is a simplified recreation of the regional performance analysis presented in the full report.
The HealthPlus audit showed clear differences in profitability across regions. Asia recorded the highest profit margin, followed closely by Europe, while North America stood out as the weakest-performing region with negative profitability. This comparison helps highlight where the business is performing efficiently and where operational or pricing issues may be reducing overall returns.




KPI Dashboards: Developed visual summaries of Total Revenue (125M) and Profit (20.7M) to track global performance.
Segment Analysis: Evaluated profitability by category, such as Education versus Travel, and by region, such as Europe versus North America.
Trend Analysis: Conducted a COVID-19 recovery assessment to model profit dynamics between 2016 and 2024.




The audit highlighted key opportunities for growth and areas where profitability could be improved. It provided a clearer understanding of category and regional performance, helping support more informed business and operational decisions.
← Back to Projects

title: Fraud Pattern Recognition and Mitigation Analysis
text:
Download Report


This project involved a detailed analysis of a fraud victim dataset to identify key demographic and behavioural patterns associated with fraudulent transactions.
By examining 500 transaction records, the study aimed to develop data-driven strategies to mitigate fraud risk and improve customer satisfaction, particularly for vulnerable groups.



The chart below recreates one of the main findings from the fraud analysis by showing how fraud prevalence differed across selected demographic groups.









This visualisation is a simplified recreation of the demographic patterns identified in the full report.
The analysis suggested that fraud was more prevalent among female customers, lower-income groups, and older customers, while higher-income groups showed lower levels of fraud exposure. These findings are important because they show that fraud risk is not distributed evenly across the dataset. Recognising these demographic patterns can help organisations design more targeted fraud prevention strategies and improve protection for more vulnerable customer groups.




Data Processing: Excel was used for data cleaning, including rounding age variables and standardising numerical features such as credit scores and income for analysis.
Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA): Python was used to generate visualisations including histograms and correlation matrices to identify trends.
Statistical Techniques: Applied descriptive statistics and outlier flagging to isolate high-risk “extreme” transactions.




The analysis identified patterns that can help organisations better understand fraud exposure and respond more effectively. The project supports more targeted fraud mitigation strategies and contributes to improving protection for higher-risk customer groups.
← Back to Projects

title: Reflections
text:
This page brings together selected written work that reflects my academic development, personal growth, and evolving interests across strategy, consumer behaviour, and broader social issues.




This portfolio reflects on key ideas from the Strategic Thinking module, including the dual process model, psychological safety, virtual reality learning, and my wider development across the semester.
It highlights how my thinking has evolved around decision-making, team dynamics, negotiation, and self-awareness.
View PDF





This piece explores the factors that influence brand loyalty, including psychological, social, cultural, personal, and economic influences.
It reflects my interest in consumer behaviour and how perception, identity, and experience shape decision-making.
View PDF





This reflection looks at how everyday habits can be shaped by products and services, using online shopping and digital engagement as an example.
It helped me think more critically about habit formation, environment, and repeated consumer behaviour.
View PDF





This piece reflects on my year abroad in Barcelona, focusing on cultural learning, language development, academic adjustment, and personal growth.
It captures an important stage in my development, particularly in terms of independence, adaptability, and confidence.
View PDF





This piece explores the social impact of Profit and Sustainability Rules in English football, arguing that financial sustainability should not come at the cost of identity, staff welfare, and community connection.
It reflects both my interest in football and my ability to connect broader strategic and social ideas to current issues.
View PDF

title: Media & Interests
text:
Alongside my academic work, I enjoy engaging with films, TV shows, and books.
I strongly believe that engaging with media, especially books and films, is important for intellectual development and helps people think more creatively and understand different perspectives.









Us — dir. Jordan Peele
A film I really admire for its originality, symbolism, and social commentary. It combines psychological tension with deeper themes around identity and class.








Game of Thrones
I enjoy how it explores power, leadership, and strategy through complex characters and political conflict.








The Seven Husbands of Evelyn Hugo
A compelling story about identity, ambition, and reinvention, with strong character development.






























































I see media not just as entertainment, but as a way of engaging with ideas, behaviour, and perspective.
Many of the films, shows, and books I enjoy explore themes such as: - strategy and decision-making
- leadership and power
- identity and human behaviour
These ideas connect strongly with my academic interests in business analytics and help me think more critically about real-world situations.

The best-case scenario reflects stronger digital integration, stable investment, and improved resilience, while the worst-case scenario reflects weaker coordination, lower investment, and greater long-term challenges. Presenting the scenarios visually helps show how strategic choices made today can shape very different outcomes for the future of Gort.

title: HealthPlus International: Global Sales & Profitability Audit
text:
The chart below recreates one of the main regional insights from the HealthPlus International audit by comparing profit margin performance across key regions.









This visualisation is a simplified recreation of the regional performance analysis presented in the full report.
The HealthPlus audit showed clear differences in profitability across regions. Asia recorded the highest profit margin, followed closely by Europe, while North America stood out as the weakest-performing region with negative profitability. This comparison helps highlight where the business is performing efficiently and where operational or pricing issues may be reducing overall returns.
